themes.exampleQuote: A perfect speakeasy vibe with the best cocktails I've had in Pittsburgh.
A superb riff on a Sazerac/Manhattan/old fashioned. Slightly sweet, little liquorice flavor, booze heavy.
Alicia was one of the best bartenders I've had and definitely deserves the bar manager position.
We were informed that we had to leave because there is a 90-minute time limit, only mentioned in the menu in very small print.
I have tried to get in a few times but it is always super packed.

notable_quote: I will be making the drive from Cleveland JUST for this bar in the near future.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the moody botanical aesthetic, named bartenders, and cocktails built around specific flavor profiles; the recurring friction is a 90-minute table limit that feels punishing when guests discover it mid-round rather than on arrival.
quarter_narrative: Commerce Bar enters Spring 2026 as a genuinely word-of-mouth-driven room: 83 percent of its 106 Google reviews are five stars, and the most detailed ones read like cocktail notes rather than casual check-ins. That depth of customer feeling is real and earned. The gap is in how little of it circulates beyond Google: one Instagram post in the last 30 days, no reels, a 0.72 percent engagement rate, no website or phone number on its Google listing, and zero indexed TikTok coverage from outside creators. Family leader Hidden Harbor is pulling ahead specifically on social momentum, which is the axis where Commerce is most exposed. The path to closing that gap runs through its own listing hygiene first, then through the social record that matches what its most loyal customers are already saying out loud.
tldr_read: Deep review warmth, nearly invisible social presence. Neighborhood Staples, #2 in Pittsburgh Bars.
tldr_meaning: The review signal is already there; the rank ceiling is set by a Google listing missing basic contact information and an Instagram that is not keeping pace with the bar's actual reputation.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Second In Pittsburgh Bars On Review Depth Alone. The Listing Has No Website, No Phone.

playbook.headline: Complete the Google Business listing today
playbook.action: Add a website URL and phone number to the Google listing, which currently shows neither, closing the most direct path visitors use to confirm hours and book.

playbook.headline: Post one reel per week for six weeks
playbook.action: Commerce has posted one time in 30 days with zero reels; a candlelit cocktail pour or a close-up of the cheese board jam would cost nothing and directly address the 0.72 percent engagement rate.

playbook.headline: Surface the 90-minute policy clearly
playbook.action: The single harshest review hinges entirely on a policy surprise at the table; moving that disclosure to a verbal mention at seating would neutralize the most predictable source of negative sentiment.
